readingOrder.emptyState: No chapters yet. Create chapters to set their reading order.
readingOrder.confirmMessage: This will reorder all {{count}} chapters based on act and storyline structure. Continue?
emptyState.title: How should your story flow?
emptyState.subtitle:
Behind every page-turner is a structure the reader never sees — but always feels.
Pick a proven blueprint. We'll show you exactly how it works.
emptyState.hint: This is a starting point, not a contract. Rename, rearrange, and reshape everything later.
emptyState.template.three_act.name: Beginning · Middle · End
emptyState.template.three_act.descriptio: The simplest and most popular structure. Your story builds to a turning point, then races to the finish. Works for almost any genre.
emptyState.template.three_act.books: The Hunger Games · The Great Gatsby
emptyState.template.five_act.name: The Dramatic Arc
emptyState.template.five_act.description: Five phases that mirror how tension naturally rises and falls. Gives your story more breathing room between the big moments.
emptyState.template.heros_journey.name: The Quest
emptyState.template.heros_journey.descri: Your protagonist leaves their world, faces trials, and returns transformed. The oldest storytelling pattern in human history.
emptyState.template.heros_journey.books: Harry Potter · Lord of the Rings
emptyState.template.save_the_cat.name: The Page-Turner
emptyState.template.save_the_cat.descrip: 15 carefully timed beats that keep readers hooked. Originally designed for screenplays, now the secret weapon for fast-paced novels.
emptyState.template.save_the_cat.books: Gone Girl · The Da Vinci Code
emptyState.template.story_circle.name: The Character Arc
emptyState.template.story_circle.descrip: Eight simple steps that track how your character changes. Starts with who they are, ends with who they become. Beautifully minimal.
wizard.summary: Every beat is customizable. Rename, reorder, or add more as your story evolves.
wizard.tagline.three_act: The most intuitive story shape — hook them early, raise the stakes, deliver the payoff.
wizard.tagline.five_act: Let tension build slowly, then release it. More room for subplots, revelations, and quiet power.
wizard.tagline.heros_journey: Call to adventure, trials, transformation. The shape of myths — and of stories readers never forget.
wizard.tagline.save_the_cat: 15 precisely timed beats that create unputdownable momentum. Born in Hollywood, perfected in fiction.
wizard.tagline.story_circle: Who is your character at the start? Who are they at the end? Eight steps between those two truths.
wizard.provenIn.three_act: The Hunger Games  ·  The Great Gatsby
wizard.provenIn.heros_journey: Harry Potter  ·  The Lord of the Rings
wizard.provenIn.save_the_cat: Gone Girl  ·  The Da Vinci Code
wizard.example.three_act.act1: Katniss volunteers for Prim and enters the Capitol's world.
wizard.example.three_act.act2: The arena begins. Rue dies. Survival becomes personal.
wizard.example.three_act.act3: The berries defy the Capitol. Two victors go home forever changed.
wizard.example.five_act.act1: Feuding families. Romeo and Juliet meet at the Capulet ball.
wizard.example.five_act.act2: Secret marriage. Family hatred escalates through street brawls.
wizard.example.five_act.act3: Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished from Verona.
wizard.example.five_act.act4: Juliet fakes her death. The message never reaches Romeo.
wizard.example.five_act.act5: Both lovers die. The feuding families reconcile over their graves.
wizard.example.heros_journey.act1: Harry lives with the Dursleys, gets his Hogwarts letter, and meets Hagrid.
wizard.example.heros_journey.act2: Hogwarts begins. Friends, Quidditch, and Voldemort in the underground chambers.
wizard.example.heros_journey.act3: Voldemort defeated. The Stone destroyed. Harry returns home, forever changed.
wizard.example.save_the_cat.act1: Nick and Amy's strained marriage. Amy vanishes on their anniversary.
wizard.example.save_the_cat.act2: Nick is the prime suspect. Amy's diary manipulates everyone. The frame job is revealed.
wizard.example.save_the_cat.act3: Nick plays the media. Amy returns — trapping him forever.
wizard.example.story_circle.act1: Bilbo is a comfortable hobbit. Gandalf arrives. He leaves the Shire for the wild.
wizard.example.story_circle.act2: The Arkenstone found. Thorin betrayed. Battle won. Bilbo returns home transformed.
